markers: Content is served through `components/IOCModalApp.js` and the IOC API routes

summary.overall: This is an IOC teaching app centered on surgical service-line overviews and histologic-pattern teaching modals. The migratable educational payload is concentrated in JSON data files; there is no standalone algorithm library and no project-owned image corpus.

lectures.note: High-level IOC overviews with principles, key points, and triad summaries.

images.note: No project-owned educational image assets found.

tutorials.note: Detailed teaching modals for histologic patterns, including case points, sketch guidance, pitfalls, and actions.

notes: The root app and bundled upgrade both expose IOC modal flows, but the content lives in the `data/overview` and `data/entity` folders.
No image directory or standalone media set was present outside generated build artifacts and dependencies.

recommendation: Migrate the JSON data folders first and treat the React/Express shell as presentation infrastructure. If the target project needs lecture/tutor content, the overview files are the strongest lecture candidates and the entity files are the strongest tutorial candidates.
